terms.definition: Binary classifier that finds a max-margin separating hyperplane wᵀx + b = 0; prediction via sign(wᵀx + b). Canonical home for max-margin classification in classical ML.
Classifier trained to maximize the geometric margin 2/||w||₂ between parallel margin hyperplanes wᵀx + b = ±1. Canonical definition in this topic.
Training example with non-zero dual weight αᵢ that lies on or inside the margin (hard margin: yᵢ(wᵀxᵢ + b) = 1; soft margin: may include violators). Canonical definition in this topic.
SVM formulation allowing slack ξᵢ ≥ 0 when yᵢ(wᵀxᵢ + b) < 1, trading margin width against misclassification via penalty C Σξᵢ.
Soft-margin trade-off: large C penalizes slack heavily (narrow margin, fewer errors); small C allows wider margin with more violations. Canonical tuning knob defined in this topic.
yᵢ(wᵀxᵢ + b) — signed distance from the hyperplane in score units; hard-margin SVM requires this ≥ 1 for all training points.
yᵢ(wᵀxᵢ + b) / ||w||₂ — perpendicular distance from xᵢ to the hyperplane; max-margin SVM maximizes the minimum geometric margin over training points.
